introSection: Zach's Zoomer is a wooden roller coaster located at Michigan's Adventure in Muskegon, Michigan, USA. It opened on July 22, 1994.

sections.title: Etymology
sections.content: Zach's Zoomer was named for for the grandson of park owner Roger Jourden, Zachery Mark.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Zach's Zoomer has a height of, a drop of and a max speed of.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: Zach's Zoomer's train is built by Philadelphia Toboggan Coasters, Inc.

sections.title: Incident
sections.content: A 38-year-old woman fell from the ride and was critically injured on June 30, 2000. Park management speculated that the woman was not seated properly when she was ejected from the train.

fullText:
Zach's Zoomer is a wooden roller coaster located at Michigan's Adventure in Muskegon, Michigan, USA. It opened on July 22, 1994.

Zach's Zoomer was named for for the grandson of park owner Roger Jourden, Zachery Mark.

Zach's Zoomer has a height of, a drop of and a max speed of.

Zach's Zoomer's train is built by Philadelphia Toboggan Coasters, Inc.

A 38-year-old woman fell from the ride and was critically injured on June 30, 2000. Park management speculated that the woman was not seated properly when she was ejected from the train.
